title: Listening - Part 4: A Mobile Bookshop
description: You will hear part of a radio interview with Maya Patel, who runs a mobile bookshop. For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

content.audio_script: Presenter: Today we’re talking to Maya Patel, who runs a bookshop from a brightly painted van and visits villages across the county. Maya, why a bookshop on wheels? || Maya: I’d worked in publishing, so books were always part of my life, but the van wasn’t a romantic dream I’d had for years. [24]When the library in my parents’ village closed, I noticed older neighbours had to make a long bus journey simply to choose a book[/24]. I started wondering whether the books could travel to them instead. I bought a delivery van second-hand and, rather nervously, began visiting three villages on Saturdays. || Presenter: Was preparing the van straightforward? || Maya: Not at all. I imagined putting up lots of shelves and filling them immediately. Then a mechanic explained that the weight would be unsafe, particularly on narrow country roads. [25]I had to redesign the inside with lighter shelving and carry fewer books than I’d originally planned[/25]. It was disappointing at first, but it taught me to select stock more carefully. || Presenter: How do you decide what to carry? || Maya: At the beginning, I chose books I personally loved, which wasn’t always sensible. A beautiful travel book may sit there for months, while somebody is desperately waiting for the next crime novel in a series. [26]Now I keep a notebook of customer requests, and those comments influence most of what I order[/26]. I still introduce new authors, but I listen first. || Presenter: What has surprised you most? || Maya: I expected people to arrive, buy books and go home. Instead, the van often becomes a meeting place. [27]Customers recommend books to one another and sometimes remain outside chatting long after they have chosen something[/27]. That social side matters in villages where there are fewer shops than there used to be. || Presenter: Do you sell online too? || Maya: I don’t post many books, because large companies already do that efficiently. My website has a different purpose. [28]People can check my route and reserve a book so I can bring it to their village on my next visit[/28]. That saves me carrying items nobody wants and means customers aren’t disappointed. || Presenter: What is most difficult? || Maya: Winter. People think it’s the cold inside the van, but I have a good heater. [29]Snow or flooding can force me to change a route with very little warning, even when customers are waiting[/29]. I now send messages before setting off and never promise to drive if the roads are risky. || Presenter: Would you like a permanent shop one day? || Maya: I’d rather keep travelling. [30]My next aim is to hold small reading events in village halls, inviting local writers to meet readers[/30]. A larger shop would mean staying in one place, and reaching different communities is the whole reason I do this.

task.title: Part 4
task.instruction: For questions 24–30, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

questions.question: Why did Maya first decide to start her mobile bookshop?

options.A: She wanted to promote books she had published.
options.B: She saw that local people had difficulty reaching books.
options.C: She was offered an inexpensive vehicle.

questions.explanation: Maya says the idea began after a village library closed and residents had to travel a long way to choose books.

questions.question: What problem did Maya have when preparing the van?

options.A: The original interior design would have been too heavy.
options.B: She was unable to find anyone to repair it.
options.C: There was not enough space for customers to enter.

questions.explanation: A mechanic warned that full heavy shelves would be unsafe, so she used lighter shelving and fewer books.

questions.question: How does Maya mainly select books now?

options.B: By choosing authors she personally admires.
options.C: By responding to what customers ask for.

questions.explanation: She keeps a notebook of requests and says those comments influence most of her orders.

questions.question: What has Maya found unexpected about the business?

options.A: People enjoy meeting and talking around the van.
options.B: Customers usually buy several books at once.
options.C: Visitors travel from cities to find her van.

questions.explanation: She says customers recommend books to one another and stay chatting outside.

questions.question: What is the main function of Maya's website?

options.A: To send books to customers throughout the country.
options.B: To let people reserve books for a later visit.
options.C: To publish interviews with local authors.

questions.explanation: Customers can check her route and reserve a title for her next visit.

questions.question: What causes Maya most difficulty in winter?

options.B: Getting new books delivered on time.
options.C: Having to alter visits because of road conditions.

questions.explanation: Snow or flooding can mean she has to change her route at short notice.

questions.question: What does Maya hope to do next?

options.A: Open a shop in one of the villages.
options.B: Organise events with writers for local readers.
options.C: Employ another driver for longer routes.

questions.explanation: She plans to hold reading events in village halls with local writers.
